objections.title: AC-C2-02 test-writer.md positive must_contain_all still gameable — 4 of 5 literals already exist in schema block 80-127; only 'root index' is net-new canary
objections.evidence: QA grep against current /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/agents/test-writer.md: 'tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json' matches at lines 80, 82, 90 (schema block, already present); 'kind": "index"' matches at line 119; 'tasks": [' matches at line 120; 'per-task manifest' matches at line 82 (schema-block prose explanation); 'root index' returns 0 hits. Therefore 4 of 5 positive literals are satisfied today by the untouched schema block. After Dev deletes the 5 must_not_contain target lines (139/181/183/198/216), only the new 'root index' literal forces any net-new wording — and Dev could add it as a single bare phrase in a code comment, satisfying the AC mechanically without performing the Section 7 P1.2 'Update test-writer.md ... in lockstep' Procedure-section (lines 174-186) + Output JSON Report (lines 191-209) + Quality Checklist (lines 213-221) rewrite.
objections.consequence: Dev could pass AC-C2-02 test-writer.md sub-check without rewriting the procedural sentences with new Option-(b) wording. The Procedure section could end up with empty/missing instructions after deletion of the 5 anchors. Section 7 P1.2 binding remediation partially unenforced.
objections.proposed_fix: Replace the bare file-level positive literals with SECTION-SCOPED required new fragments. (a) Drop 'per-task manifest' as a must_contain_all literal (it already exists in schema block 80-127). (b) Add as must_contain_all new exact-wording fragments that MUST appear in the rewritten Procedure section: 'Read existing tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json', 'Write tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json'. (c) Add as must_contain_all: '"manifest_path": "tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json"' (replacement for deleted line 198 JSON example). (d) Add as must_contain_all: '"root_index_path": "tests/generated/manifest.json"' or equivalent literal demonstrating the Output JSON Report template now distinguishes per-task path from root index. (e) Keep 'root index' as prose canary. (f) Regenerate ac_uid since the check object changes. With these fragments, Dev cannot complete the AC without actually adding new procedural wording to the Procedure/Output/Checklist sections.
objections.spec_clause: spec-20260518-225715.md Section 7 P1.2 'Update test-writer.md AND qa.md AND commands/dev.md verification step in lockstep' + context.json codex_consult.findings[2] (codex F3)
objections.codex_concurrence: Codex sustained as BLOCKER. Codex's proposed fix matches QA's: 'replace bare file-level literals with section-scoped required replacements for ## Procedure, ## Output JSON Report, and ## Quality Checklist. Require exact new fragments such as: Read existing tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json, Write tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json, Update tests/generated/manifest.json as the root index, "manifest_path": "tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json", "root_index_path": "tests/generated/manifest.json".'

objections.title: Ticket prose for agents/qa.md sub-check mentions only 1 of 2 must_not_contain literals — prose↔JSON drift
objections.evidence: AC JSON acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json AC-C2-02.check.files_and_patterns[1] (agents/qa.md) has must_not_contain: ['pytest tests/generated/ -q', 'Read `tests/generated/manifest.json`. For every `active_tests[]` entry']. Ticket prose at ticket-20260519-211515-CYCLE2-round2.md:225 reads: 'agents/qa.md: must NOT contain the legacy pytest tests/generated/ -q literal currently at line ~995'. The second must_not_contain literal (the codex F4 root-iteration anti-pattern at line 994) is NOT mentioned in the ticket prose. Dev who reads only the ticket would not know that the line 994 root-iteration must also be removed.
objections.consequence: Dev could leave the line 994 root-iteration form intact, only fix the line 995 pytest line, and the AC check would FAIL (because the AC JSON has both must_not_contain entries) — but Dev wouldn't know WHY from the ticket prose alone. Wasted iteration cycles. If Dev only reads ticket prose without consulting AC JSON, Dev under-implements the codex F4 anti-pattern remediation.
objections.proposed_fix: Update ticket prose AC-C2-02 agents/qa.md bullet to: 'agents/qa.md: must NOT contain (i) the legacy `pytest tests/generated/ -q` literal currently at line 995, NOR (ii) the codex F4 root-iteration anti-pattern `Read tests/generated/manifest.json. For every active_tests[] entry` currently at line 994; must contain the scoped `pytest tests/generated/<task_id>/` invocation literal AND the literal schema-field names `root_index_path` AND `per_task_manifest_path` AND `current task_id` (so Phase 5 actually wires Option-(b) read pattern: read global index then find current task_id then read per-task manifest_path then verify only that manifest).' Ticket-only change; no ac_uid regeneration needed.
objections.spec_clause: spec-20260518-225715.md Section 7 P1.2 + acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json AC-C2-02 must_not_contain[1] (codex F4)
objections.codex_concurrence: Codex sustained as MAJOR. Codex provided identical proposed prose.

objections.title: Ticket prose for commands/dev.md sub-check is too vague vs AC JSON's specific lockstep enforcement — prose↔JSON drift
objections.evidence: AC JSON acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json AC-C2-02.check.files_and_patterns[2] (commands/dev.md) has must_not_contain: ['verify on disk that `tests/generated/manifest.json` AND `docs/dev/test-writer-report-<task_id>.json` BOTH exist'] AND must_contain_all: ['tests/generated/manifest.json', 'tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json', 'docs/dev/test-writer-report-<task_id>.json', 'all three']. Ticket prose at ticket-20260519-211515-CYCLE2-round2.md:226 reads only: 'commands/dev.md: must reference the Option-(b) read pattern (root index then per-task manifest) consistently with the other two files.' This prose does NOT mention (i) the must_not_contain on line 632 verbatim wording, (ii) the literal 'all three' Section 7 P1.2 lockstep enforcement, (iii) the 3-artifact list.
objections.consequence: Dev who reads only the ticket might add a generic Option-(b) reference without removing the line 632 two-artifact verification AND without adding the three-artifact verification with 'all three' literal. The codex F5 lockstep enforcement (Section 7 P1.2 'in lockstep' across all 3 files) would be under-implemented in commands/dev.md while still passing the literal AC check.
objections.proposed_fix: Update ticket prose AC-C2-02 commands/dev.md bullet to: 'commands/dev.md: must NOT contain the legacy verification line currently at :632 (`verify on disk that tests/generated/manifest.json AND docs/dev/test-writer-report-<task_id>.json BOTH exist`); must contain (i) `tests/generated/manifest.json` (root index path), (ii) `tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json` (per-task manifest path), (iii) `docs/dev/test-writer-report-<task_id>.json` (report path), AND (iv) the literal phrase `all three` indicating the verification step now requires all three artifacts (Section 7 P1.2 lockstep enforcement, codex F5).' Ticket-only change; no ac_uid regeneration needed.
objections.spec_clause: spec-20260518-225715.md Section 7 P1.2 'in lockstep' + acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json AC-C2-02 commands/dev.md sub-check (codex F5)
objections.codex_concurrence: Codex sustained as MAJOR. Codex's proposed fix matches: 'commands/dev.md must_not_contain "verify on disk that tests/generated/manifest.json AND docs/dev/test-writer-report-<task_id>.json BOTH exist" and must require all three artifact literals.'
